prompt: Build a Sports Venue Commerce & Knowledge Hub that lets an on-prem operator run a local sports retail catalog, manage perishable inventory, publish aggregated sports news briefs for customers and staff, and deliver competency assessments for employees, all from a single browser-based interface. Users include an Administrator who configures the system, a Content Editor who curates news and product content, an Inventory Manager who controls stock and expiration, and a Trainer who assembles and grades assessments; regular Staff Users can search, reserve, and complete assigned tests. The UI is built with server-rendered pages enhanced by HTMX so search, filtering, and inline edits feel instantaneous without a heavy frontend framework: users can run multi-field combined queries across products, SKUs, news items, and questions with pagination defaulting to 25 results per page, sort by recency or price, and use fuzzy matching with highlighted hits. Frequently used filter sets can be saved and pinned as one-click quick searches. Product management supports multi-variant SKUs, publish/unpublish, primary image upload, rich-text descriptions, tags/categories, tiered pricing (for example, $79.00 standard, $69.00 for 10+ units), and purchase limits such as a maximum of 5 units per order for controlled items; bulk import/export uses local CSV files. Inventory is tracked by warehouse, bin, and batch, with FEFO picking recommendations and expiration alerts at 30 days and 7 days before expiry; stock counts record variances and require a reason note when variance exceeds 2% or 10 units. The backend uses Flask to enforce workflows, validate inputs, and expose REST-style endpoints consumed by HTMX partial requests, while SQLite persists all data locally so the system remains fully functional with no internet connection. Sports news ingestion is implemented as scheduled local jobs that parse RSS/Atom/XML/JSON files dropped into a watched folder (or captured HTML snapshots), applying admin-managed allow/block lists and per-source parsing rules; each run writes monitoring logs, retries failures up to 3 times with exponential backoff (1, 5, 15 minutes), and quarantines malformed files based on format validation and file hash tracking to prevent duplicate imports. Inventory reservation is implemented as order locking with a 20-minute hold that auto-releases if not confirmed, with concurrency control to prevent negative available stock and an overbooking guard that rejects holds once a configurable buffer is exceeded. The pricing and inventory rules engine supports date-range rate plans, markups/discounts, minimum booking length (default 60 minutes), and advance-booking windows (minimum 2 hours, maximum 60 days), applying rules deterministically on the server for every quote and hold. Security is offline-first: username/password only with minimum 12 characters, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes, salted hashing, and masking of sensitive fields at rest; sessions use short-lived JWTs (30 minutes) with optional refresh up to 8 hours. APIs require HMAC-SHA256 request signing with a timestamp skew limit of 5 minutes and nonce anti-replay stored for 24 hours, plus rate limiting (60 requests/minute/user), CSRF protection, output encoding and rich-text sanitization, and rule-based anomaly flags (for example, repeated failed logins plus rapid search bursts) recorded in an audit log for administrator review.
